body:
## Goal
Provide a minimal, dependency-free Node.js HTTP server that answers GET / with an HTML "Hello, World!" page so the project has a runnable, self-verifying baseline.

## Spec
Single ESM source file `server.js`. Create the server with the built-in `node:http` module — no external web framework and no added runtime dependency.

Read the listen port from `process.env.PORT`, coercing with `Number.parseInt(..., 10)`; fall back to `3000` when the variable is unset or non-numeric.

Request handling: for `GET /`, respond `200` with header `Content-Type: text/html` and a body containing the exact substring `Hello, World!`. Other methods/paths respond `404`.

Structure startup so the request handler is importable by the test while `listen()` is only invoked when the file is run as the entry module (guard on `import.meta.url` matching the process entry, or export a `createServer`/handler and start under the guard).

Add a `start` script to package.json (`"start": "node server.js"`) while preserving the existing `type: module` and the `typecheck`, `lint`, and `test` scripts.

A `node --test` test in `server.test.js` starts the server on an ephemeral port (`listen(0)`) and asserts the response status is 200, the `Content-Type` header is `text/html`, and the body includes `Hello, World!`; it also asserts the default-port resolution logic yields 3000 when `PORT` is absent.

## Changes
- `server.js` — creates
- `package.json` — refactors-existing
- `server.test.js` — creates

## Acceptance
- [ ] AC-1: server.js is the only non-test source file added and imports only Node built-ins (no runtime dependency added to package.json dependencies): the server sources HTTP via `node:http`.
- [ ] AC-2: The listen port resolves from process.env.PORT and defaults to 3000 when PORT is unset or non-numeric (asserted by server.test.js).
- [ ] AC-3: A GET request to / returns HTTP status 200 with a Content-Type response header of text/html and a response body containing the exact substring "Hello, World!" (asserted by server.test.js).
- [ ] AC-4: package.json defines an npm start script that launches the server: `node -e "process.exit(require('./package.json').scripts.start && require('./package.json').scripts.start.includes('server.js') ? 0 : 1)"` exits 0.
- [ ] AC-5: package.json declares no new entries under dependencies: `node -e "process.exit(Object.keys(require('./package.json').dependencies||{}).length === 0 ? 0 : 1)"` exits 0.
- [ ] AC-6: `npm test` exits 0 with the server test present.

## Verify
- npm test (unit)
- npm run lint (validate)
- node -e "process.exit(require('./package.json').scripts.start ? 0 : 1)" (validate)

## References
- `README.md` — exists

## Non-Goals
- No external web framework (Express/Fastify/etc.) or any added runtime dependency.
- No routing beyond GET / (other paths may return 404).
- No TLS/HTTPS, structured logging, or process-manager integration.

<!-- meta: {"reason_to_exist":"Deliver the sole runnable HTTP surface (GET / -> HTML \"Hello, World!\") as one cohesive slice: a single source file, its npm start script, and its test are one coherent change with one reason to exist (c: cohesion — a single-file server plus its start script and test cannot be split without fragmenting one capability)."} -->

<!-- plan-story: fe08879622c31a89 -->
title: Minimal dependency-free Node.js HTTP server serving Hello, World!
